<!-- Generated by the spec-test-coverage-audit workflow (63 agents, 446 requirements). counts={'covered': 336, 'partial': 100, 'uncovered': 10} antiGaps=67. A point-in-time audit; verify against the suite before acting. -->

# Spec↔Test Coverage Audit — photos-cartographer

**Scope:** 446 mapped requirement clauses across all 4 spec files (`photos-shared-contract.md`, prep, geotag, merge). The pipeline's stated USP is *safety on irreplaceable originals*, so this audit weights **anti-behaviors** (must-refuse / must-abort / no-clobber / leaves-untouched) above happy-path positives.

---

## 1. Executive summary

| Status | Count | % of 446 |
|---|---:|---:|
| Covered | 336 | 75.3% |
| Partial | 100 | 22.4% |
| Uncovered | 10 | 2.2% |

### Anti-behavior coverage (the safety surface) — called out separately

| Type | Total | Covered | Partial | Uncovered |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| **Anti** (refuse/abort/no-clobber/untouched) | 247 | 180 (72.9%) | 59 (23.9%) | **8 (3.2%)** |
| Positive (happy-path) | 199 | 156 (78.4%) | 41 (20.6%) | 2 (1.0%) |

**Negative-path coverage is materially weaker than positive-path coverage**, which is the wrong way round for a safety-first pipeline:

- Anti-behaviors are **less often fully covered** (72.9% vs 78.4%) and **three times as likely to be uncovered** (3.2% vs 1.0%).
- **8 of the 10 fully-uncovered clauses are anti-behaviors, and 5 of those are HIGH criticality.** Every one is a "the tool must NOT do the dangerous thing" guarantee that no test exercises.
- The dominant *partial* pattern across the suite is real and worth naming: the **positive half is asserted but the negative/anti half is not**. Recurring shapes:
  - "blocks with the right message" is asserted, but **not** that *nothing was written / nothing was mutated / the file is still where it was* (e.g. `prep-dryrun-mutates-nothing-1`, `merge-precond-fail-no-output-1`, `seal-new-dump-warn-1`).
  - A **cache/index primitive** is unit-tested, but the **workflow guarantee it underwrites** (single-writer thread, fingerprint-once-per-run, regions disjoint) is never asserted (`*-single-writer-*`, `merge-collision-fingerprint-once-1`, `db-disjoint-writers-1`).
  - **Concurrency determinism** is proven for *prep planning* but **not for geotag execute or merge execute under -j** as a same-input-different-jobs equality (`exec-concurrency-no-semantic-change-1`, `merge-moveset-fixed-before-concurrency-1` is partial).
  - **"Config is read-only on the data path"** has essentially no negative test on the geotag/merge side (`config-merge-readonly-1`, `merge-config-readonly-1`).

Bottom line: the *detection/refusal* of bad state is well tested; the *non-mutation / no-side-effect* consequence of that refusal frequently is not.

---

## 2. Gap list — RANKED (safety-critical anti-behavior first)

### Tier 1 — UNCOVERED, HIGH-criticality anti-behaviors (write these first)

| # | Clause (id) | Why it matters | Suggested test (one line) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **`merge-execute-occupied-different-blocker-1`** — target found free at plan, occupied by *different content* at execute → blocker, source left in by-dest, never a clobber | This is the classic TOCTOU clobber of an irreplaceable library file; the entire "no clobber" promise hinges on the execute-time recheck, and the *different-content-appeared-after-plan* path is completely unexercised | Plan with empty lib, then drop a different-content file at the planned target before execute; assert rc 3, source preserved, library byte unchanged |
| 2 | **`merge-snapshot-required-aborts-1`** — required pre-mutation snapshot that cannot be taken aborts before any placement | If a required snapshot silently no-ops, merge could mutate the library with no rollback point — the snapshot safety net is unverified for the one phase that writes the permanent library | Configure zfs `snapshots_required` + force snapshot failure; assert status `rejected`, nothing moved into library, no seal |
| 3 | **`merge-prep-consistency-1`** — a by-dest photo not in the finalized-name set is a "re-run prep" blocker | An unrecorded by-dest photo could be merged with no audit lineage; precondition-4's blocker (`_check_prep_consistency`) has zero merge-level coverage | Place a by-dest photo absent from the handoff/photos-24 join; assert preflight blocks with "Re-run prep" and no placement |
| 4 | **`merge-config-readonly-1`** — merge data path (plan/dry-run/execute) never writes `photos-00-config.json` | A config rewrite on the merge path would corrupt the authoritative workspace config; no test compares config bytes before/after a merge run | Snapshot config bytes, run plan→dry-run→execute, assert byte-identical |
| 5 | **`prep-no-journal-replay-1`** — prep never replays the journal to continue an old plan; journal is evidence, not a resume script | Replaying a stale journal could re-apply or double-apply mutations after a crash; the no-replay guarantee is asserted nowhere | Corrupt/delete the prior run's journal, re-run prep, assert recovery still succeeds via replan-from-filesystem |

### Tier 2 — UNCOVERED, remaining

| # | Clause (id) | Why it matters | Suggested test |
|---|---|---|---|
| 6 | `prep-reserved-names-not-moved-1` (anti, med) | An as-arrived dump folder named `0-sources`/`5-photos-by-date` must be treated as the managed folder, not moved beneath itself (data-shuffling hazard) | Uninitialized ws with a dump folder named `5-photos-by-date`; assert no init-move-beneath-itself op |
| 7 | `prep-root-block-strict-dotfiles-1` (anti, med) | A plain root dotfile on an initialized ws must hard-block; strictness unasserted | Place `.DS_Store` at root of an initialized ws; assert "Misplaced entry at workspace root" |
| 8 | `seal-prune-exception-1` / `prep-seal-prune-exempt-1` (pos, high) | `prune-quarantine` is the *sole* operation permitted on a sealed workspace; the carve-out is unexercised (and `prep-seal-prune-exempt-1` is HIGH) | Seal ws, run `prune-quarantine`, assert it runs (not exit-2 SEALED) |
| 9 | `prep-quarantine-restore-normal-1` (pos, med) | A file restored out of quarantine must re-flow as a normal dump (no un-quarantine path) | Move a quarantined file back into 0-sources, re-prep, assert re-evaluated/re-quarantined under new plan_id |
| 10 | `prep-missing-metadata-no-special-path-1` (anti, low) | No special re-import path from 2-missing-metadata | Move a fixed file from 2-missing-metadata→0-sources, re-prep, assert ordinary routing to 5-photos-by-date |

### Tier 3 — PARTIAL, HIGH-criticality anti-behaviors (the "blocks but no-mutation-not-asserted" cluster)

These all assert the refusal/detection but **not** the safety consequence. Grouped by the missing assertion.

**Missing "nothing was written / mutated / relocated" on a refusal path:**
- `prep-dryrun-mutates-nothing-1` — assert fs/DB/cache byte-identical before vs after a dry-run.
- `merge-precond-fail-no-output-1` — assert `photos-31-merge-summary.json` is **absent** on a precondition stop.
- `merge-no-stale-summary-1` — assert a stale-plan rejection writes **no** summary at all.
- `seal-new-dump-warn-1` / `merge-sealed-newdump-warn-1` — assert the dumped file still exists at its original path after the sealed warning (left exactly where it is).
- `merge-lock-failure-no-mutation-1` — assert library byte-unchanged when either lock can't be acquired.
- `config-merge-readonly-1` — assert geotag & merge plan/dry-run/execute leave config byte-unchanged.
- `prep-quarantine-no-auto-delete-1` — seed a populated quarantine, run prep, assert contents survive.
- `prep-prune-no-managed-1` — assert managed folders + 6-photos-by-dest survive a prune run.

**Missing "ordering / single-threaded-before-concurrency" assertion:**
- `exec-concurrency-no-semantic-change-1` — run the *same geotag plan* under `-j1` vs `-jN`, assert identical photos-25 + file tree.
- `merge-moveset-fixed-before-concurrency-1` — assert snapshot + revalidation precede the parallel move pass (sequencing probe).
- `exec-single-writer-journal-1` — assert journal/SQLite/ledger writes never originate from worker threads; pre-state pass completes before the write pass.
- `prep-cache-single-writer-1` / `prep-db-disjoint-writers-1` / `prep-execute-no-patch-stale-1` — assert no two phases write the same rows / no worker-thread DB write.

**Missing "library/by-dest untouched / never-follows / never-fixes" assertion:**
- `merge-noclobber-1` — execute-time re-verify where target became occupied by different content after planning (overlaps Tier-1 #1).
- `merge-marker-check-before-liblock-1` — run locked workflow against a marker-less library_root, assert **no** `.photos-merge.lock` was dropped.
- `gate-no-symlinks-by-dest-1` — place a file/dir symlink **under 6-photos-by-dest**, assert blocked and not followed (currently only root symlinks tested).
- `reprep-geotag-no-fix-1` — assert cache/handoff/by-dest byte-unchanged after geotag hits the pending-move gate (geotag never does move recognition).
- `move-reevaluate-dest-1` — end-to-end: move a file between destinations, assert geotag re-evaluates under the NEW dest's tz+offset, old values not carried.
- `time-name-never-reverted-1` — assert withdrawing a decision produces no time/filename revert and never relocates across destinations.
- `merge-no-time-gps-changes-1` — assert EXIF time/GPS tags byte-identical before/after a merge placement (not just file bytes).
- `merge-execute-no-rederive-1` — doctor a saved photos-30 disposition/resolved_name, assert execute applies it verbatim.

**Missing "no mutation during planning" assertion:**
- `prep-plan-nonmutating-1` — assert `plan()` alone leaves 0-sources/SQLite/handoff untouched.
- `merge-planning-nonmutating-1` — assert by-dest + library byte-unchanged and no 31/35/journal/seal after `plan`.

**Other HIGH anti partials:**
- `lock-failfast-1` — assert no plan/dry-run/journal artifact produced on the lock-contention path.
- `lock-covers-whole-run-1` — assert lock contention refuses for `execute` and `finalize`, not just `plan`.
- `merge-seal-blocks-future-scripts-1` — covers refusal for all 3 phases; missing the prune-quarantine sole-exception (see #8).
- `merge-journal-confirmed-only-1` — read the merge journal after a partial run, assert only the confirmed move present, failed item absent.
- `merge-config-validated-1` — run a bad placement_policy/collision_policy through the merge data path, assert blocker + no summary + no placement.
- `prep-path-conflict-bydest-1` — exercise §11.2 *case-insensitive* collision against an existing by-dest path → safe-alternative or block.
- `prep-scan-skip-control-1` — assert `.git` and `.photos-ingest-quarantine/` subtrees are skipped during the media scan.
- `prep-video-in-photoband-block-1` — add the 6-photos-by-dest video case (only the 5-band is tested) and assert "produces no plan".
- `prep-init-exclude-managed-1` — direct assertion that init's move excludes `.git`/`.photos-ingest-quarantine` and never moves a structure folder into 0-sources.
- `prep-idempotent-recognize-correct-1` — isolated negatives for "don't re-normalize already-normalized mutable extension" and "don't re-quarantine quarantined dup".
- `prep-replan-not-resume-1` — leave a stale plan artifact on disk, assert it is discarded/superseded, not resumed/patched.
- `artifact-location-control-dir-1` — scan 6-photos-by-dest after a geotag run, assert no artifact landed there.
- `exec-tool-failure-not-silent-1` — assert (a) file byte-unchanged after exiftool failure and (b) a subsequent run re-applies (doesn't treat as no-op).

### Tier 4 — PARTIAL, HIGH-criticality positive guarantees

- `exec-confirm-lock-1` — assert geotag `execute` confirms/holds the lock and fails-fast when another run holds it.
- `prep-execute-confirm-lock-sentinel-1` — assert execute confirms the lock is held (currently execute runs lock-free in tests).
- `atomic-ops-1` / `exec-atomic-ops-1` — crash-injection mid same-fs rename / mid metadata write asserting pre-state-or-post-state (currently atomicity only inferred from the OS/exiftool flag).
- `plan-depends-on-22-1` — assert `drift_validation` (photos-22) is in photos-24 `depends_on` and that withdrawing a drift confirmation after planning makes execute reject.
- `utc-fingerprint-includes-22-1` — the zero-scrub-only case (photos-22 hash changes, no row delta) to prove the SHA-256 fold-in, not just per-row deltas.
- `utc-stale-recompute-1` — enumerate each named trigger (camera_time_policy / camera_group_key_version / photos-21 sha / prep_cache_fingerprint / metadata_field_set_version) as individually restaling resolved UTC.
- `preflight-validate-deps-1` — add negative tests for prep cache/SQLite **schema version** mismatch and **metadata_field_set_version** mismatch (no rejection test exists for either).
- `prep-depends-on-block-1` — assert the **plan** artifact and **prep audit log** each carry a `depends_on` block (only journal/handoff/cache asserted).
- `prep-sentinel-last-1` — fail a real init op and assert the guard sentinel is withheld.
- `prep-preplog-on-success-only-1` — run a failing execute and assert `photos-15-prep-log.json` is absent (success-gate, like the handoff has).

### Tier 5 — remaining MEDIUM/LOW partials (lower priority)

~50 medium/low partials remain, mostly "primitive tested, workflow integration not asserted" or "message-text not pinned." Representative clusters worth a sweep when convenient:
- **Cache-once / no-library-scan** (`merge-fp-cache-once-1`, `merge-collision-fingerprint-once-1`, `merge-no-library-scan-1`, `merge-no-read-when-free-1`): add call-count spies on `_fingerprint_library_file`.
- **Backup-nothing on unchanged** (`canon-unchanged-nobackup-1`): feed byte-identical content through `write_versioned_json`, assert `bak=None`.
- **Config path kind/boundary** (`val-paths-1`): file-where-dir-expected + boundary-escape + whitespace-only config paths.
- **Cross-phase snapshot isolation** (`db-snapshot-clobber-refresh-1`): re-run one phase, assert another phase's `.db` snapshot byte-unchanged.
- Message-guidance text not asserted: `prep-root-block-message-1`, `prep-missing-folder-message-1`, `merge-dryrun-no-plan-stop-1`, `gate-sources-empty-1` (residual-tolerance half).

## 3. Per-spec-file coverage

| Spec file | Clauses | Covered | Partial | Uncovered | Covered % | Uncovered (criticality) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| `photos-shared-contract.md` | 90 | 72 | 17 | 1 | 80.0% | `seal-prune-exception-1` (med) |
| prep (`photos-1-prep`) | 163 | 122 | 36 | 5 | 74.8% | `prep-no-journal-replay-1` (high), `prep-reserved-names-not-moved-1` (med), `prep-root-block-strict-dotfiles-1` (med), `prep-quarantine-restore-normal-1` (med), `prep-missing-metadata-no-special-path-1` (low) |
| geotag (`photos-2-geotag`) | 109 | 89 | 20 | 0 | 81.7% | — (none uncovered; 20 partials) |
| merge (`photos-3-merge`) | 84 | 53 | 27 | 4 | 63.1% | `merge-prep-consistency-1`, `merge-config-readonly-1`, `merge-snapshot-required-aborts-1`, `merge-execute-occupied-different-blocker-1` (all high) |
| **Total** | **446** | **336** | **100** | **10** | **75.3%** | |

**Merge is the weakest spec by every measure** — lowest covered % (63.1%), highest partial+uncovered share (37%), and **all 4 of its uncovered clauses are HIGH-criticality anti-behaviors guarding the permanent library** (the one place mutations are irreversible into a non-quarantined store). This is the inverse of where coverage should be densest. Geotag has zero uncovered but 20 partials concentrated in concurrency/ordering/fingerprint-fold-in assertions.

---

## 4. Requirements where the audit itself was uncertain

These mappings carry caveats that affect whether "covered" is truly safe to trust:

- **`merge-summary-on-success-only-1`** and **`merge-status-values-1`** — the audit flags a **spec↔code contradiction**, not just a test gap. The clause says the summary is written "only on success" and lists `failed`/`rejected` as statuses, but the implementation writes `photos-31` on partial/rejected too, never emits literal `failed`, and the stale-plan `rejected` path writes no summary at all. Marked covered/partial against *actual* behavior — **the spec should be reconciled before writing tests to the literal wording.** (Same applies to `summary-25-status-1`: code emits only `success`/`partial`/`rejected`, never `failed`.)
- **`atomic-ops-1` / `atomic-metadata-write-1` / `exec-atomic-ops-1`** — marked covered/partial but **atomicity-under-interruption is delegated to the OS (`renameat2`) and exiftool (`-overwrite_original`) and asserted only via the flag/mechanism**, never via crash injection. "Covered" here means "the atomic primitive is invoked," not "a torn write is provably impossible."
- **`db-snapshot-consistent-atomic-1`** — covered, but the explicit caveat is that no test injects a fault *mid-VACUUM/mid-rename*; the "never a corrupt file from an interrupted capture" clause rests on the atomic-rename mechanism + no-temp-residue, not a mid-capture crash sim.
- **`merge-execute-occupied-identical-1`** vs **`merge-execute-occupied-different-blocker-1`** — the *identical-content* execute-time-occupied branch is covered, but its *different-content* sibling is fully uncovered; reviewers may mistake the covered one for covering the dangerous case. They are different code paths.
- **Test-name approximations** — several mappings note the exact test name is approximate (e.g. `prep-quarantine-recoverable-1`, `utc-after-time-decisions-1`, `prep-execute-revalidate-1`, `prep-prune-explicit-1`). The asserted *behavior* was located by line/content, but a name drift could mean the cited test moved; verify names before relying on them as regression anchors.
- **Source-grep "tests"** — `prep-scope-no-time-gps-1`, `prep-nongoals-1`, `gpx-prep-unaware-1`, `prep-metadata-passive-1` lean partly on static source-text greps (no `apply_time_sync`, no `GeotagWorkflow` class) rather than behavioral assertions. These are brittle (a rename defeats them) and weaker than the runtime `metadata_write`-rejection guards they sit alongside.
- **`exec-confirm-lock-1` / `prep-execute-confirm-lock-sentinel-1`** — the spec says execute "confirms the lock is held," but the implementation deliberately holds the lock once in `main()` and execute does **not** re-acquire/verify it; tests run execute lock-free. Whether this satisfies the clause is a spec-interpretation question, not purely a coverage gap.
